commit.message:
Add duplex spring conversion to Spring Builder

New feature: when converting a spring, the tool now calculates all
viable duplex options (6" outer + 3-3/4" inner nested) alongside
standard single-coil conversions.

Backend:
- spring_calculator_service: new _calculate_duplex_conversions()
  method finds all viable outer/inner wire combos for a given IPPT,
  sorted shortest first, limited to top 15 options
- spring_builder_service: adds catalog matching (part numbers +
  in-stock status) for each duplex option, plus cone sets for
  both outer and inner coils

Frontend:
- Conversion tab now shows a "Duplex Conversion Options" table
  below the standard conversion results
- Table shows: outer wire/length/part#, inner wire/length/part#,
  length saving vs original, and stock status (In Stock / Partial /
  Special Order)
- Example: .421 x 6 x 58 converts to .312 x 6 x 20.5" outer +
  .250 x 3.75 x 20.3" inner — saving 38" of shaft space

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
CRITICAL: Fix Bug 16 — NUCLEAR_TP + universal peak_pct tracking

BUG 16: Community at +12.7% and Alex at +42.3% sat open without
selling. Proactive TPs at +3%/+5% didn't fire. peak_pct was 0.0
for ALL positions because it was only updated inside GRAD block.

FIX 1 — UNIVERSAL PEAK TRACKING:
Added `if p.pct_change > p.peak_pct: p.peak_pct = p.pct_change`
BEFORE all strategy-specific logic. Now ALL positions track peaks.

FIX 2 — NUCLEAR_TP at +5%:
If pct_change >= 5.0, sell IMMEDIATELY. This fires BEFORE any
strategy elif chain, BEFORE hard caps, BEFORE everything except
moonbag check. CANNOT be bypassed by any elif/exception.

Community +12.7% → would now exit as NUCLEAR_TP(+12.7%) instantly.
Alex +42.3% → would now exit as NUCLEAR_TP(+42.3%) instantly.

FIX 3 — HFT belt-and-suspenders (Bug 13):
Added return at top of process_new_coin too. Both entry paths blocked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: 5 protezioni agente vocale

1. APPUNTAMENTO DUPLICATO: check hasFutureAppointment() prima di creare
   - Se esiste: "Ha gia un appuntamento per [data]. Vuole confermarlo?"
2. RETURNING LEAD: check tag "Lead Vocale Web" via telefono
   - Se trovato: "Bentornato! Come posso aiutarla?"
   - Mostra appuntamento esistente se presente
3. RATE LIMITING: Redis-based
   - 50 req/ora per IP
   - 3 mutazioni/ora per conversazione (complete_flow, save_client_data)
4. VALIDAZIONE DATI:
   - Telefono italiano: +39 3xx xxx xxxx
   - Nome: almeno nome + cognome, caratteri italiani
5. TURN COUNTER con urgency:
   - normal (1-7), high (8-11), critical (12+)
   - Dopo 8 turni: propone appuntamento
   - Dopo 12 turni: forza verso prenotazione

commit.message:
fire WS event handlers as background tasks to unblock the event loop

The WS listener was processing events sequentially — each handler had to
complete before the next message (or any interaction callback) could run.
When multiple events arrived in quick succession (match_found →
both_confirmed → talk_channel_created × 2), the accumulated fetch_user
and DM calls blocked the event loop long enough for pending Discord
interactions to expire their 3-second acknowledgment window, causing
"Unknown interaction" errors on defer().

Switching to asyncio.create_task() lets WS handlers run concurrently
without blocking the message loop or interaction dispatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add two-pass Office attachment parsing for email (--deep flag)

Pass 1 (pure): eml_parser preserves base64 data + extension for
DOCX/PPTX/XLSX attachments in attachment-data/attachment-ext blocks.

Pass 2 (effectful): main.ail resolves Office attachments when --deep
flag is set — decodes base64, writes temp file, runs existing Office
parser (parseDocx/parseXlsx/parsePptx), replaces data with parsed
content blocks, cleans up temp file.

Tested with a synthetic DOCX containing paragraphs + table. The DOCX
table (Region/Revenue with 3 rows) is fully extracted inline.

19/19 email gap checks at 100% coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Migrate to asyncio: async RPC handlers, httpx, and async embedding providers

- All @isabelle_remote_procedure handlers are now async def
- requests.post replaced with httpx.AsyncClient
- time.sleep replaced with asyncio.sleep
- threading.local replaced with contextvars.ContextVar
- semantic_interpretation: removed nested asyncio.run(), await directly
- semantic_embedding: Embedding_Provider methods async, _embed_cached
  backend parameter typed as Awaitable
- semantics: _conn_semantic_vector_store async, _resolve_embedding_model
  uses connection.config_lookup_sync for sync resolution
- hover: fixed null check ordering (pretty_unicode after None check)
- collect_semantics: uses async with Client(), Client._write

Fix zero entries: remove 3s momentum recheck + loosen avg buy filter

SCALP_WATCH had zero entries because:
1. 3-second momentum recheck added 3s delay + rejected tokens dropping
   >1% in those 3 seconds. Combined with other filters = nothing passed.
2. $50 avg buy filter too strict for midday volume

Fixes:
- Removed 3s momentum verification (DEXScreener 5min data is fresh enough)
- Avg buy: $50 → $20 (still filters wash trading bot buys)

The proactive TP tiers + ratchet + pattern detection handle exit quality.
Entry just needs to find tokens — exit logic handles the rest.

commit.message:
implementar policies para Income/Expense con matriz de permisos por rol

- Crear TransactionPolicyTrait con lógica compartida
- Crear IncomePolicy y ExpensePolicy usando el trait
- Director: sin restricción en crear/editar/eliminar
- Gerente/Administrador: editar/eliminar cualquier registro solo del mismo día
- Supervisor/Controlador: editar solo registros propios del mismo día, no eliminan
- Todos pueden crear siempre
- Gate::before no hace bypass para Income/Expense (las policies se evalúan)
- Vistas usan @can/@endcan en vez de canBeEditedBy/canBeDeletedBy
- Rutas de create/edit sin restricción de rol (la policy controla)

commit.message:
Quick fix: disable HFT, 10min max hold, Geyser 403 stop-after-3

FIX 1 — HFT DISABLED: return at top of open_sim_position.
0% WR across every session. $100+ sim losses. Exits still work (Rule 2).

FIX 2 — 10-MINUTE MAX HOLD: any position held > 600s force-closes.
MEZo was stuck 2.2 hours at +2.2%. rocky stuck 2.3 hours at -4.5%.
Now: MAX_HOLD fires before strategy-specific caps, catches everything.
If profitable (>1%): exits as MAX_HOLD_TP (win).

FIX 3 — GEYSER 403: stops after 3 consecutive 403 errors instead of
retrying forever. HFT is disabled anyway so Geyser is low priority.

FIX 4 — DUPLICATE CHECK: verified GRID uses per-level keys,
MICRO uses per-mint keys. _grid_bought_this_cycle prevents 5-at-once.

ERROR_LOG updated with bugs 13-15 (HFT persist, stuck positions, dupes).

commit.message:
feat: LLM-based Intent Expander for natural language tool routing

Adds a pre-processing step that uses Claude Haiku (~800ms) to understand
what a non-technical user actually wants and map it to the correct tools.
Small business owners can now say "help me beat my competitors" or
"I need a marketing campaign" and get the full tool suite activated.

Architecture:
- New file: src/lib/orchestrator/intent-expander.ts
  - Takes user's natural language + Brand DNA context
  - Knows the complete tool catalog (50 tools, what each does)
  - Understands tool composition (campaign = research + content + video + etc.)
  - Understands phase ordering (research before content, leads before outreach)
  - Returns structured JSON: tools[], scrapeUrls[], isComplex, reasoning
  - Falls back gracefully to regex if LLM call fails

- Modified: src/app/api/orchestrator/chat/route.ts
  - Two-layer intent detection: LLM expander (primary) + regex (fallback)
  - Results merged via union — both layers contribute tool selections
  - Existing PHASE_ORDER and nudge loop unchanged (enforces execution order)
  - Logged as [IntentExpander] for observability

The regex layer stays as a fast deterministic check (~0ms). The LLM layer
adds intelligence for vague/natural requests that regex can't parse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
AIレビュー機能追加、インラインタイトル編集、マップピンチズーム対応

- AIレビュー: 旅程の抜け漏れを分析し破線カードUIで提案表示（ボトムシート）
- インラインタイトル編集: ヘッダー・ホーム画面カード両方で直接編集可能
- マップ: トラックパッドピンチイン/アウトでズーム（カーソル位置基準）

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add chain_of_thought prompt strategy.

Introduces a COT_SYSTEM_PROMPT that instructs the model to reason through
the query (tables, joins, filters, aggregations) before writing SQL, using
a structured Reasoning: / SQL: output format.

extract_sql is updated to handle both plain and CoT outputs (backward-
compatible): detects the SQL: marker and extracts everything after it.
A new _extract_reasoning helper pulls the reasoning block out of CoT
responses and stores it in AgentResult.reasoning and task metadata, making
the model's thinking visible in inspect view and Langfuse traces.

max_tokens bumped to 1024 for CoT to give the model room to reason.

Run with:
  python scripts/run_eval.py --strategy chain_of_thought
  python scripts/run_eval.py --strategies zero_shot few_shot_dynamic chain_of_thought

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add attachment chain parsing and thread reconstruction for email

Attachment chain parsing: text-based attachments (CSV, HTML, Markdown,
nested EML) are decoded and parsed inline as SectionBlock with kind
"attachment". Binary attachments stay as placeholders. Forwarded emails
(message/rfc822) are parsed recursively.

Thread reconstruction: parseMboxThreaded groups MBOX messages into
conversation threads using Message-ID/In-Reply-To/References headers.
Output as SectionBlock with kind "thread" containing thread-message
children. Quote stripping removes "> " prefixed lines and attribution.

17/17 email gap checks at 100% coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix VAT calculation: derive from actual order total, not hardcoded price

- total_excl = total_incl / (1 + VAT_RATE)
- vat = VAT_RATE / (1 + VAT_RATE) * total_incl
- unit_excl derived from actual unit price
- Removed hardcoded BOOK_PRICE_INCL/EXCL constants

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: compact steps section to force PDF to exactly 2 pages

html2pdf uses canvas slicing, not CSS page-break rules, so the only
way to prevent orphaned headings and eliminate page 3 is to reduce
total content height. Compacted steps: gap 12->8, marker 43->36,
title 20->18px, desc 18->16px, tag 14->13px, footer 17->15px.
Reclaims ~120px, ensuring all 8 steps + footer fit on page 2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

MICRO_SCALP: fast micro-trades on CLMM tokens + fee model upgrade

ALL 10 CHANGES FROM MICRO_SCALP_PROMPT IMPLEMENTED:

1. CLMM fee model: 0.05% for established tokens (was 0.25%)
   JUP,RAY,ORCA,PYTH,HNT,JTO,BONK,wETH route through CLMM pools
   Round-trip: 0.10% instead of 0.50% = 5x more profit per trade

2. MICRO_SCALP strategy: 7 tokens, 3s polling, 10-60s holds
   Entry: detect 0.3%+ dip from 5-reading high, confirm bounce
   Exit: +1.5% TP, -0.5% SL, 30s flat, 60s hard cap
   Position: 0.25 SOL per trade, max 8 parallel

3. CLMM math verified (Rule 5):
   +1.5% move - 0.10% RT fees = +1.40% net = +$0.28 per win
   -0.5% move - 0.10% fees = -0.60% = -$0.12 per loss
   At 40% WR: profitable. At 50% WR: very profitable.

4. Circuit breaker: 5 consecutive losses = 5 min pause
5. Duplicate position bug: verified _grid_bought_this_cycle working
6. calc_sim_pnl now accepts clmm parameter for correct fee routing
7. MICRO added to STRAT_COLORS, _hard_caps, adaptive trail, task list

commit.message:
fix(home): cards-carousel grid on desktop, flex carousel on mobile

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix semantic_judge calibration: normalize rows and ground the prompt.

Two targeted fixes based on observed false-partial verdicts:

1. Pre-normalize actual_rows before serializing for the judge — converts
   datetime objects to ISO strings and rounds floats, so the judge sees
   clean values instead of Python reprs (datetime.date(2024, 3, 5) vs
   "2024-03-05" was triggering spurious "partial" verdicts on q02/q10).

2. Strengthen the judge system prompt with explicit grounding rules: do not
   infer missing rows from world knowledge, treat equivalent value
   representations as equal, ignore column aliases and row order.
   Fixes the q13 hallucination where the judge claimed August was missing
   despite it not appearing in the reference result either.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Grading tab to Robusta section with RC_Grading_Feed.xlsx visuals

- New load_rc_grading() loads RC/Grading/RC_Grading_Feed.xlsx, converts Excel serial dates
- Robusta section now has two tabs: Certified Stocks (existing) + Grading (new)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: change ALWAYS_ESCALATE from hard-block to passthrough in hooks

The PreToolUse hook endpoint was returning decision:"block" for
destructive patterns (git push main, rm -rf, etc.), which permanently
blocks the tool call with no override. Changed to "passthrough" so
Claude Code falls through to its normal approval prompt, letting the
operator approve manually. Drones still cannot auto-approve these
patterns — they never reach the rules engine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(home): fix carousel header visibility — wrap cards in .cards-carousel, keep header outside scroll

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address security issues in triggers, protocol, and Cast tools (#1)

* fix: improve error handling across castApiPost, engine restart, main loop, and thread polling

- Add try/catch inside castApiPost so callers (including permissionRelay) don't need to handle fetch errors
- Verify process spawned successfully after restart(), set proc to null on failure
- Wrap engine.send() in try/catch in main loop to prevent crash if subprocess died
- Add debug logging to thread polling catch block (was silently swallowing errors)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address security issues in triggers, Cast tools, and protocol

- Use crypto.timingSafeEqual for Bearer token comparison in trigger
  server to prevent timing side-channel attacks
- Escape trigger source attribute with escapeXmlAttr to prevent
  prompt injection via crafted source values
- Cache Cast API config at module level to avoid reading ~/.clair-castrc
  from disk on every tool call
- Wrap channel message content in CDATA when it contains </channel> to
  prevent XML injection that breaks parsing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: complete security fixes and address review feedback

- CDATA-wrap trigger prompts to prevent XML injection via </trigger>
- Gate token counting by isFinalResult to prevent double-counting
- Re-enqueue unsent messages during model restart instead of dropping
- Extract shared Cast config to src/cast/config.ts (deduplicate)
- Extract wrapCdata() helper for reuse across protocol and triggers
- Remove dead proc null-check in conversation restart (Bun.spawn throws)
- Add 9 tests: CDATA wrapping, source escaping, wrong-length token

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent eval data leakage in MedQA-USMLE split

Previously the HF 'test' split (~1,273 samples) was repurposed as the
val_loader during Stage 1 training. Evaluating on it afterward would
be contaminated because checkpoint selection was done against it.

Fix: carve 10% from the HF 'train' split as validation during training.
Keep the HF 'test' split completely isolated — loaded in the DatasetDict
but never passed to any DataLoader in training scripts.

Also document the evaluation isolation strategy for all three datasets
in the module docstring.

fix: Allow multiple critics per outlet for long-run WE shows

The outlet-level dedup was keeping only the most recent review per outlet,
discarding earlier reviews by different critics (e.g., Billington 1986 vs
Winship 2021 at The Guardian for Phantom). For long-run WE shows, key by
outlet+critic so different critics both survive dedup.

Leads: handleDelete now calls /api/leads DELETE endpoint (admin SDK)
instead of client-side FirestoreService.delete which was silently failing
due to Firestore security rules blocking client SDK writes. Same pattern
applied for contacts (/api/contacts/{id}) and deals (/api/deals/{id}).

Sequences: Added full delete capability — confirmation dialog with
cancel/delete buttons, FirestoreService.delete call, optimistic UI
update. Previously sequences had no delete functionality at all.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Add process.exit(0) to prevent Playwright hang in poller and status scripts

Both scripts load Playwright via the require chain (→ gather-reviews.js
→ require('playwright')). Playwright's background processes keep the Node
event loop alive indefinitely. Without explicit process.exit(0) on
success, CI hangs after finding reviews and never reaches git push.

gather-reviews.js already had this fix. Now all three scripts match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Broadwayscore

commit.message:
Add Claude Code intro article in Japanese

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Respect criticNameManual flag in collect-review-texts

When criticNameManual is set on a review source file, the author
enrichment step will not overwrite the critic name. This prevents
merge conflicts and pipeline re-runs from reverting manually
verified critic names back to "Unknown".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Cap ticket buttons at 4, tone down lottery/rush styling

- Max 4 ticket buttons (3 platforms + Official Site, or 4 platforms)
  to prevent button overflow on shows with many links
- Lottery/Rush changed from bright green (bg-emerald-500/15, text-emerald-400)
  to subdued gray (bg-surface-overlay, text-gray-500) — it was the most
  visually compelling button but generates zero affiliate revenue
- Removed Trailer button from mobile layout to save space for ticket links
- Affiliate links (amber) are now clearly the most clickable buttons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
